arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

# Try to download the debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using SSQP convention
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try the ELF debug file
curl -sfL -o /tmp/libcoreclr.so.dbg "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" && echo "Downloaded debug symbols (_.debug)" && file /tmp/libcoreclr.so.dbg || echo "Failed _.debug download"

# Also try the full binary with symbols
curl -sfL -o /tmp/libcoreclr.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" && echo "Downloaded libcoreclr.so" && file /tmp/libcoreclr.so || echo "Failed libcoreclr.so download"

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames. The tombstone shows frames from `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. 

To symbolicate these frames, I need to:
1. Find the debug symbols for this specific build of `libcoreclr.so`
2. Use the BuildId to locate the correct symbol file
3. Use a tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses

The BuildId is `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. 

Microsoft publishes symbol files for .NET runtime builds, so I can try downloading them from Microsoft's symbol server. The symbol server typically uses URLs like `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/<buildid>/<filename>`, or for ELF files, it might use the debuginfod protocol or SSQP. I can get the .NET runtime symbols from either Microsoft's symbol server or NuGet runtime packages.

For native Linux/Android symbols, Microsoft uses a specific SSQP key convention where the URL format for ELF binaries includes the build ID, so I should try constructing the proper URL with the `elf-buildid-` prefix and the actual build ID from the binary.

Let me start by checking what tools are available on the system, then attempt to download the symbols from the Microsoft server and use either llvm-symbolizer or addr2line to resolve the stack trace addresses.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

# Try to download the debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using SSQP convention
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try the ELF debug file
curl -sfL -o /tmp/libcoreclr.so.dbg "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" && echo "Downloaded debug symbols (_.debug)" && file /tmp/libcoreclr.so.dbg || echo "Failed _.debug download"

# Also try the full binary with symbols
curl -sfL -o /tmp/libcoreclr.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" && echo "Downloaded libcoreclr.so" && file /tmp/libcoreclr.so || echo "Failed libcoreclr.so download"
